title: Monsoon Palace
tagline: Monsoon Palace — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: The Monsoon Palace, also known as the Sajjan Garh Palace, is a hilltop palatial residence in the city of Udaipur, Rajasthan in India, overlooking the Fateh Sagar Lake.
overview.description: The Monsoon Palace, also known as the Sajjan Garh Palace, is a hilltop palatial residence in the city of Udaipur, Rajasthan in India, overlooking the Fateh Sagar Lake. It is named Sajjangarh after Maharana Sajjan Singh (1874–1884) of the Mewar dynasty, whom it was built for in 1884. The palace offers a panoramic view of the city's lakes, palaces and surrounding countryside. It was built chiefly to watch the monsoon clouds; hence, appropriately, it is popularly known as Monsoon Palace.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sajjangarh Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Sajjangarh Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Udaipur District of Rajasthan in the southern Aravalli Hills. The area of the sanctuary is 5.19 square kilometres.

topPlaces.name: Sajjangarh Biological Park
topPlaces.description: Sajjangarh Biological Park is a zoological garden located in Udaipur, Rajasthan, India. This biological park is situated just beneath the Monsoon Palace (also known as Sajjangarh Palace), around 4 km from the city center.

topPlaces.name: Agasthyavanam Biological Park
topPlaces.description: Agasthyavanam Biological Park is a protected area in the Western Ghats in Kerala State, India. The park is located within Vazhichal Panchiyat and lies between the Neyyar and Peppara Wildlife Sanctuaries which covers an area of about 31 square kilometres (12 sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Lake Badi
topPlaces.description: Lake Badi is an artificial freshwater lake situated in Udaipur city in the Indian state of Rajasthan.

topPlaces.name: Fateh Sagar Lake
topPlaces.description: Fateh Sagar Lake is situated in the city of Udaipur in the Indian state of Rajasthan. It is an artificial lake named after Maharana Fateh Singh of Udaipur and Mewar, constructed north-west of Udaipur, to the north of Lake Pichola in the 1680s.

topPlaces.name: Taj Aravali Resort & Spa
topPlaces.description: Taj Aravali Resort & Spa is a luxury resort located near Udaipur, Rajasthan, at the foothills of the Aravalli Range. Spread over 27 acres, the resort opened in 2018 and offers 176 rooms, suites, and tents designed with regional decor elements.

topPlaces.name: Pratap Park
topPlaces.description: Pratap Park is a new garden and open gym in Udaipur, Rajasthan, India. It is situated near the bank of Pichola Lake.

topPlaces.name: Udaipur Solar Observatory
topPlaces.description: The Udaipur Solar Observatory (USO) is a solar observatory located in Udaipur, Rajasthan, India on an island in the Fateh Sagar Lake. The sky conditions at Udaipur are quite favourable for solar observations.

items.note: Sajjangarh Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Udaipur District of Rajasthan in the southern Aravalli Hills. The area of the sanctuary is 5.19 square kilometres.

Sajjangarh Biological Park is a zoological garden located in Udaipur, Rajasthan, India. This biological park is situated just beneath the Monsoon Palace (also known as Sajjangarh Palace), around 4 km from the city center.

Agasthyavanam Biological Park is a protected area in the Western Ghats in Kerala State, India. The park is located within Vazhichal Panchiyat and lies between the Neyyar and Peppara Wildlife Sanctuaries which covers an area of about 31 square kilometres (12 sq mi).
Lake Badi is an artificial freshwater lake situated in Udaipur city in the Indian state of Rajasthan.

Fateh Sagar Lake is situated in the city of Udaipur in the Indian state of Rajasthan. It is an artificial lake named after Maharana Fateh Singh of Udaipur and Mewar, constructed north-west of Udaipur, to the north of Lake Pichola in the 1680s.

Taj Aravali Resort & Spa is a luxury resort located near Udaipur, Rajasthan, at the foothills of the Aravalli Range. Spread over 27 acres, the resort opened in 2018 and offers 176 rooms, suites, and tents designed with regional decor elements.
Pratap Park is a new garden and open gym in Udaipur, Rajasthan, India. It is situated near the bank of Pichola Lake.

The Udaipur Solar Observatory (USO) is a solar observatory located in Udaipur, Rajasthan, India on an island in the Fateh Sagar Lake. The sky conditions at Udaipur are quite favourable for solar observations.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Monsoon Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Monsoon Palace is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Monsoon Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharana Pratap Airport, Udaipur (26 km) and the nearest railway station is Udaipur City (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Monsoon Palace?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Monsoon Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Monsoon Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Monsoon Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Monsoon Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sajjangarh Wildlife Sanctuary, Sajjangarh Biological Park, Agasthyavanam Biological Park, Lake Badi, Fateh Sagar Lake.
